title: Transient element exposed API — stable read/event surface across self-replacement (prior-art survey)
summary: Grounds decision #1961 (the stable read/event surface a transient/self-replacing custom element exposes). A transient (A-family) element (fui:blocks/transient/TransientElement.ts) replaces itself with a native element on upgrade via this.replaceWith(); the host that erased itself becomes a DETACHED node. Standards survey (WHATWG DOM/HTML, CSSOM-View, WAI-ARIA APG) confirms: methods on a detached node never throw — they no-op against the orphan subtree (Node.isConnected===false is the only liveness signal); getBoundingClientRect returns a zero rect because the element is NOT RENDERED (cause, not 'disconnected'); focus() bails because a non-rendered element is not a focusable area; dispatchEvent still fires own-subtree listeners but loses propagation to the live document; and crucially custom-element 'replace yourself' is NOT a spec-sanctioned pattern (HTML warns against tree manipulation in reactions, connectedCallback can fire >1x and with isConnected false). No native change event exists on <button>. Prior-art scan: NO mainstream library does runtime self-erasure — HTML web components (Keith), Lit, Shoelace and Web Awesome PERSIST the host as the API surface; compile-away frameworks (Enhance, WebC, Astro islands) unwrap at build/server time; htmx swaps wholesale and answers lost listeners with event delegation on a stable ancestor. The standards-blessed customized built-in (is='button') is foreclosed by WebKit's permanent refusal, which is precisely why self-replacement gets hand-rolled. Toggle-state exposure survey (APG, Shoelace, Spectrum, Primer, Carbon, GoogleChromeLabs): APG's blessed baseline is native click + read aria-pressed with NO custom event (aria-pressed is the single stable reflected key); design systems that add a custom change event keep the authored prop REFLECTED and tell consumers to ignore click (double-fire trap). Key reframe: the rename selected->aria-pressed / value->data-value is the actual anti-pattern, orthogonal to self-replacement — every system keeps the authored read key stable across enhancement. Local grounding (frontierui): the rename is NOT A-family-wide — only the two toggle controls (FilterChipElement, ButtonTransientElement) rename; the rest are pure-presentational (Badge/Tag/Card) or fully rebuilt (TextField/Progress/Meter/NumberInput). data-value is already a phase-agnostic convention and read-helpers already exist (isFilterChipSelected reads aria-pressed). No A-family element dispatches a CustomEvent; only B-family persistent behaviors do (step-change/tab-change, StepperBehavior.ts). Implication: scope narrows to the two toggle controls; aria-pressed is the ARIA-mandated canonical survivor key (not a fork); prior art favors making the read MECHANISM stable (stop renaming) over doc-only; a custom change event is complementary but carries the double-fire trap (coordinate with #1960 Fork 2); the 'don't self-replace / host persists' and 'customized built-in is=' alternatives are out of scope by precedent (ratified transient-family pattern; Safari) and recorded with reasons. Full grounding in reports/2026-06-29-transient-element-exposed-api.md.
